heading: Contracts to perform workloads previously performed by depot-level activities of the Department of Defense: requirement of competition
label: 2469 Contracts to perform workloads previously performed by depot-level activities of the Department of Defense: requirement of competition

L. 103–337“(a) .—The Secretary of Defense or the Secretary of a military department may not change the performance of a depot-level maintenance workload that has a value of not less than $3,000,000 and is being performed by a depot-level activity of the Department of Defense to performance by a contractor unless the Secretary uses competitive procedures for the selection of the contractor to perform such workload.Requirement for Competition“(b) A–76.—The use of Office of Management and Budget Circular A–76 shall not apply to a performance change under subsection (a).”Inapplicability of OMB Circular1993—, amended section, as amended by , (h), by designating existing provisions as subsec. (a), inserting heading, striking out “threshold” before “value”, substituting “to performance by a contractor unless the Secretary uses competitive procedures for the selection of the contractor to perform such workload” for “unless the Secretary uses competitive procedures to make the change”, and adding subsec. (b).Pub. L. 103–160, § 346Pub. L. 103–160, § 1182(a)(7), struck out “, prior to any such change,” after “Department of Defense unless”.Pub. L. 103–160, § 1182(a)(7)

children.text: merit-based selection procedures for competitions among all depot-level activities of the Department of Defense; or
competitive procedures for competitions among private and public sector entities.

children.heading: .—Scope
children.label: b .—Scope
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (c), subsection (a) applies to any depot-level maintenance and repair workload that has a value of not less than $3,000,000 (including the cost of labor and materials) and is being performed by a depot-level activity of the Department of Defense.

children.heading: .—Exception for Public-Private Partnerships
children.label: c .—Exception for Public-Private Partnerships
children.text: The requirements of subsection (a) may be waived in the case of a depot-level maintenance and repair workload that is performed at a Center of Industrial and Technical Excellence designated under subsection (a) of by a public-private partnership entered into under subsection (b) of such section consisting of a depot-level activity and a private entity.section 2474 of this title

children.heading: –76.—Inapplicability of OMB Circular A
children.label: d –76.—Inapplicability of OMB Circular A
children.text: Office of Management and Budget Circular A–76 (or any successor administrative regulation or policy) does not apply to a performance change to which subsection (a) applies.
